title: Annual growth of the gross capital formation
subtitle: Gross capital formation is the total value of investments in a country. This includes spending on new buildings, machinery, infrastructure, inventories, and other long-term assets that help produce goods and services.
caption:
Annual growth of the gross capital formation

Gross capital formation is the total value of investments in a country. This includes spending on new buildings, machinery, infrastructure, inventories, and other long-term assets that help produce goods and services.

This choropleth world map displays the annual growth of gross capital formation for the year 2023. The color scale ranges from deep red, representing a contraction of 50%, to dark blue, representing growth of 50%, with a neutral yellow indicating 0% change. The highest growth rates, falling into the darkest blue bands above 25% and 50%, are concentrated in specific regions of Africa, including Mozambique, Botswana, and the Democratic Republic of the Congo. Surrounding nations in Southern and Central Africa generally appear in lighter blue shades, indicating positive growth between 5% and 25%. In contrast, parts of South America and the Middle East display orange and red hues, signifying negative growth; Venezuela and Yemen sit in the darkest red bands, indicating a decline exceeding 25%. North America and Western Europe are predominantly colored in pale yellow and light orange, clustering near the 0% to -5% range. Russia and parts of Eastern Europe show light blue shades, suggesting modest positive growth. Large areas, including Greenland, Australia, and several countries in Africa and the Middle East, are marked with diagonal hatching, indicating that no data is available for these territories. The visual distribution highlights a divergence where specific African economies experienced substantial investment growth, while other regions faced stagnation or contraction in capital formation during this period.
